input.background: The Scandinavian simvastatin survival study (4S) was a randomized clinical trial designed to evaluate the effects of the cholesterol-lowering agent simvastatin in patients with coronary heart disease. Over 5.4 years of follow-up, the treatment group consisting of 2221 individuals experienced 111 fatal heart attacks. The placebo group of 2223 individuals experienced 189 such events.
input.question: Calculate the risks in the groups and test the difference for significance.

output.answer:
$$
\begin{aligned}
& \hat{p}_1=111 / 2221=0.049977 \
& \hat{p}_2=189 / 2223=0.085020
\end{aligned}$$

A. Hypotheses. $H_0: p_1=p_2$ versus $H_{\mathrm{a}}: p_1 \neq p_2$
B. Test statistic. $z_{\text {stat }}=4.66$
C. $P$-value. $P$ (two-tailed) $=0.0000032$ (highly significant)
D. Significance level (optional). The evidence against the null hypothesis is significant at $\alpha=0.01$.

treatment_group_size.description: The number of individuals in the treatment group.

treatment_group_events.description: The number of fatal heart attacks in the treatment group.

placebo_group_size.description: The number of individuals in the placebo group.

placebo_group_events.description: The number of fatal heart attacks in the placebo group.
